item.title: Did American-Style ‘Gentle Parenting’ Spoil French Children?
item.description: As positive parenting takes over France, one psychologist’s call for a return to discipline has set off a furious debate.

item.title: Why Is Florida Executing So Many Prisoners?
item.description: In most of the country, executions are a thing of the past. But one state has been carrying them out at a record pace.

item.title: How A.I. Might Change the Way Doctors Think
item.description: For generations, writing up a summary of a patient exam was a vital step for physicians trying to make an accurate diagnosis. What happens when A.I. does it for them?

item.title: Is a ‘Frictionless’ Society a Trap?
item.description: Eliminating whatever stands between you and a task sounds nice, but it probably has more to offer companies than users.

item.title: Has the MAHA Movement Given Up?
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his allies promised public-health libertarianism. The idea couldn’t survive once they took power.

item.title: The Perishable, Never-Ending Genius of Lionel Messi
item.description: How are his legions of fans savoring what could be the last World Cup of their idol? I dove into a sea of screaming Messis to find out.

item.title: My Sister Insists That She Can’t Work. Should I Pay Her Bills?
item.description: I want to help, but I worry financial support may enable her rather than help her.

item.title: I Think the Workmen at My Condo Are Being Exploited. What Should I Do?
item.description: Many of us suspect they are undocumented, and being underpaid.

item.title: Can You Make a Bartender Prepare Off-Menu Soft Drinks?
item.description: A ruling on a restaurant etiquette dispute.

item.title: My Aunt Pretends My Cousin Doesn’t Have a Drug Problem. Do I Have to Play Along?
item.description: I am sick of the charade we are expected to maintain around them.

item.title: How American Socialism Changed, and Stormed the Democratic Party
item.description: The movement was better at critiquing the system than reshaping it. But it has never had this much mainstream political power.

item.title: The Sorrow, Rage and Crazy Joy of the Knockout Rounds
item.description: For this phase of the competition, picture an automatic carwash made of cheese graters.

item.title: Mick Jagger Knows He May Have Played His Last Rolling Stones Show
item.description: The legendary rock star, now 82, on how fame, touring and aging have changed him.

item.title: How a Gang of Thieves Pulled Off a Multimillion-Dollar Data Center Heist
item.description: The world’s most valuable assets are stored on rows of servers in giant, anonymous buildings. And they can be stolen.
